content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

November 27, 2002

Diane M. Fishburn
Olson, Hagel & Fishburn, LLP
555 Capitol Mall, Suite 1425
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-297

Dear Ms. Fishburn:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Jack O'Connell and other candidates for statewide elective office regarding the application of certain provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ You have asked the following questions:

1. Does Government Code section 85316 apply to the campaign committees of candidates for statewide elective office in the November 5, 2002 election? If the candidates were elected to the offices they sought in the November 5, 2002 election, and their committees had no debt, may the committees continue to accept contributions after November 5, 2002?

2. If the candidates may continue to accept contributions after November 5, 2002, do any contribution limits apply to the funds raised by these committees?

These are essentially the same questions that were asked in the Fishburn Advice Letter, No. A-02-257. We anticipate that the Commission will consider these and other related issues at its December 13, 2002 meeting. Therefore, we decline to provide advice at this time.

___________________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-02-297
Page No. 2

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Holly B. Armstrong
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

HBA:jg
